name: Family Style French Toast
directions: Combine brown sugar, butter and cinnamon; spread evenly in a greasted 15 x 10 x 1 inch baking pan; set aside.
Combine eggs and milk in a shallow dish; place bread in dish and soak for 5 minutes, turning once.
Place bread over sugar miture.
Bake, uncovered at 350 degrees for 20- 25 minutes or until golden brown.
You can serve it with maple syrup but it is really good without.

name: Spiced French Toast
directions: Whisk eggs together with brown sugar and pumpkin pie spice until well blended
Coat each side of the bread slice with the mixture.
Cook in a heated pan (buttered or coated with non stick spray) at Medium heat or 325 degrees on electric skillet until both sides are crisp.
Dust with powdered sugar and serve hot.

name: Cornflake French Toast
directions: Mix together all ingredientsexcept cornflakes.
Dipp bread in egg mixture then into corn flakes.
Fry with butter until golden.

similar.name: Gluten Free Dairy Free French Toast

name: "cinnabon" French Toast
directions: Slice Cinnabon rolls in half horizontally. Place them, cut side up, on a plate and allow to dry for 20 to 30 minutes.
Combine eggs, half-and-half, vanilla extract, sugar, and Makara Cinnamon. Whisk together until all ingredients are well blended. Refrigerate until ready to use.

At serving time, preheat oven to 400 degrees F, and place Cinnabon halves in a shallow baking dish. Pour French toast batter over halves and allow to soak for 5-10 minutes until moist. You may weigh the rolls down (cover them with wax paper and place a heavy skillet on the paper). During this time to make sure batter is absorbed into the dough layers.

Heat a heavy skillet and melt butter or margarine in it. When butter is hot, place the Cinnabon halves into the pan, gooey side down. Sear until golden brown. Turn the halves over, and place the skillet in the oven. Bake 8 to 10 minutes or until rolls are puffed up and golden.
Serve with maple syrup.

name: Sticky-Bun French Toast
directions: In a small saucepan, combine 1 cup brown sugar, 1/2 cup butter and 2 tablespoons corn syrup. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until thickened.

Pour sauce into a 9x13 inch baking dish and sprinkle with 1/2 cup pecans. Place 6 slices of bread on top of the sauce, sprinkle with the remaining pecans and cover with the remaining 6 slices of bread.
Combine eggs, milk, vanilla, nutmeg, cinnamon and salt in a blender. Pour egg mixture evenly over bread slices, cover the baking dish, and refrigerate for 8 hours or overnight.

The next morning, preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C). Remove the French toast from the refrigerator 30 minutes prior to baking. Bake in preheated oven for 40 to 45 minutes, until lightly brown. Drizzle sauce over the toast right before serving.
To make the Caramel Sauce: In a small saucepan, combine 1/2 cup brown sugar, 1/4 cup butter and 1 tablespoon corn syrup. Cook until thickened, stirring constantly.

name: French Toast 2.0
directions: Combine cream cheese and sugar until creamy.
Add the Vanilla extract.
In a bowl, whisk the eggs, half & half, vanilla, salt and honey until combined.
Preheat the oven to 375°F.
Cut the bread into 1" thick slices.
Cut each slice along the crust on two sides, making a pocket.
Spoon the filling into the pockets being careful not to overfill.
Slide a couple chocolate pieces into the center of the pocket and press slightly to seal.
Soak each slice into the mixture for 30 seconds per side.
Set on a cooling rack for 2 minutes to allow full absorption.
Add butter to a skillet and melt until foamy.
Cook slices briefly until golden on each side.
Return to cooling rack.
Slide cooling rack into the oven for 10-15 minutes to bake the centers. (Put a cookie sheet underneath to catch the drippings for a less messy oven!).
Serve warm plain or with toppings such as syrup or powdered sugar.

similar.name: Gluten Free Dairy Free French Toast

name: French Toast to Go
directions: Separate buns.  With a serated knife, skim off the top or bottom crust from each of the buns.  Set aside.  Cook sausage patties according to directions.  If you have bulk sausage, form into bun size patties.  In flat bottom bowl, mix eggs, milk, sugar, salt, and vanilla.

Preheat griddle to med-low heat (or about 325°).  Lightly butter griddle.  Briefly soak each bun on both sides and place onto griddle.  Sprinkle cinnamon on the top of each of the bun.  Cook until golden brown on each side.

Take two pieces of the french toast buns and place a sausage between the buns.  You now have french toast to go.  Or, you can serve it the traditional way with butter, warmed maple syrup and powdered sugar.

name: Simple French Toast
directions: In a shallow bowl, crack eggs and pour milk. Whisk together. Put 1 tablespoon of vanilla extract in egg mixture. Mix. Set aside.
In a skillet or gridle, melt one tablespoon of butter. Put on medium heat. The Skillet is ready when butter starts to bubble.

Soak a piece of bread in egg mixture entirely on bothe sides. Put on the skillet. Flip the bread over when the side is golden brown. (approx 5 minutes) Wait another 5 minutes or uuntil the other side is golden brown.
Continue this for the next 5 pieces of bread. Turn pan off.
Enjoy! Goes well with Maple Syrup.

name: French Toast
directions: In a mixing bowl whisk half and half, eggs, honey, salt, french vanilla, cinnamon, and nutmeg.
Lay bread into the mixture and let it sit for about 5 minutes, if not fully covered flip it and let it sit another 5 minutes.
Melt a little butter in a pan and cook each slice until golden crispy on each side.
Serve with your favorite toppings. (I perfer a light sprinkling of powdered sugar and some high grade maple syrup.).

name: Julia's French Toast
directions: Whisk all ingredients, except bread, together.
Spray a skillet with pam.
Dip bread on both sides in mixture.
Brown on both sides in skillet.

name: French Toast
directions: In a large flat container, combine the Half and Half, Egg, Honey, Brown Sugar, Cinnamon, Vanilla Extract, Cloves, Nutmeg, and Agave Nectar. Stir well and spread mixture evenly across container.
Soak slices of bread in mixture, about 2 minutes on each side.
Meanwhile, heat 2 heavy skillets over medium-high heat, and spray them with the non-stick spray.

Place the slices of bread on the skillets, and keep the heat as is. Watch carefully for when to flip the bread, as it will burn easily. (The best way to tell is to look for bubbles on the sides of the bread, and the bread starting to stick to the skillet.) Flip, and cook approximately the same amount of time on the other side.

Remove from heat, and place on a large plate. Cut each piece of bread into about 5 long strips, then cut the strips in half to form 20 pieces of toast. Organize these toast pieces around the edges of a smaller plate, and place a small bowl of your favorite syrup in the middle.
Serve warm, and enjoy!

name: French Toast Loaf (Not As Eggy)
directions: Tear bread into chunks, approximately one inch or so. Divide into two bowls.
Beat eggs and milk together. Add vanilla and a sprinkle of cinnamon.
Pour egg mixture over first bowl of bread, and evenly coat pieces. You want them coated, not soaked.
Combine a few dry pieces and a few wet ones to make a layer on the bottom of two greased loaf pans or 8x8s. (you will make 3 layers in load pans or 2 in 8x8s.)
Combine sugar, cinnamon, salt, and flour. Dust over the first layer. Sprinkle with a few pecans. Repeat process, ending with cinnamon-sugar mixture and pecans on top.
Dot with butter and drizzle with maple syrup.
Bake loaves at 325 35 to 45 minutes in a preheated oven. Bake 8x8s at 350 for 35 minute.

similar.name: Gluten Free Dairy Free French Toast

name: Perfect French Toast
directions: Whisk the eggs with the sugar and flavorings, then stir in the milk.
Pour the batter into a pie plate and put in two pieces of the bread.
Let stand for 3 minutes, then turn it over and lets stand again to absorb the batter.
Press on it with your fingers. You can tell if it's still dry.
Melt a little butter in a large nonstick skillet. When its bubbling, pick up the bread and put it in the skillet.
Cook both sides until nicely browned.
Repeat with the remaining bread and batter.

name: 2014 Memorial Day Buttermilk French Toast
directions: Whisk all ingredients.
Over medium heat, lightly grease pan or griddle with 1 tablespoon butter or less.
Dip each slice of bread into the egg mixture, flip to coat each side. Do this quickly, so as not to completely soak the bread.
Place on pan over medium heat for approx 2-3 min per side. Flip to repeat on reverse side.
Slice on a diagonal and serve with powdered sugar or syrup.

name: Gluten Free Dairy Free French Toast
directions: In a large bowl or pie pan, mix eggs, fruit puree, milk, cinnamon, nutmeg and sugar.
Take a piece of bread and dip both sides in the egg mixture, draining off extra coating.
Heat a skillet to medium high heat and place slice(s) in skillet, cooking for 1-2 minutes on each side, until golden brown; remove to a plate in the oven to keep warm, while cooking rest of slices.
Top with syrup, honey, powdered sugar or fruit.

name: French Toast
directions: Whisk together cinnamon, nutmeg, sugar, eggs, milk and vanilla.
Heat frying pan over medium-high heat.
Dip each slice of bread into the egg mixture and flip it so that both sides of the bread are coated.
Fry until golden brown, then flip and cook the other side.

name: Eggnog Spiced French Toast
directions: Melt butter in a pan.
Combine eggs, milk, vanilla and spices.
Dip a piece of bread into the mixture and lightly coat with mixture. Fry in a pan until browned.
Serve with maple syrup.

name: Eggnog Spiced French Toast
directions: Melt butter in a pan.
Combine eggs, milk, vanilla and spices.
Dip a piece of bread into the mixture and lightly coat with mixture. Fry in a pan until browned.
Serve with maple syrup.
